Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: From the log, we can observe the following patterns: 1. Every case starts with OR, then MS, then QS, then AS. This sequence is consistent in all cases.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 2. After the first AS, there is an IQC in cases 1,2,3,5. However, in case 4, there is no IQC and no rework. So, we have a choice: either proceed with IQC or skip it.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. In cases 1,2,3,5, after IQC, we see AS again (rework) and then sometimes IQC again (case 3 and 5 have multiple IQC-AS loops). This suggests a loop for rework after IQC.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: ReworkLoop = * ( IQC, AS ) This means: execute IQC, then either exit the loop, or execute AS and then IQC again, repeating until the loop is exited. After the first AS, we have a choice: either skip the rework and go to TST, or enter rework.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 5. After TST, we see that in cases 1,3,5 there is an RT (Re-Testing) followed by TST again. This is a loop for re-testing. In case 2, there is no RT, so it goes straight to PK after TST.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: TestingLoop = * ( RT, TST ) After the first TST, we have a choice: either skip the re-testing and go to PK, or enter the re-testing loop.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But note that the TestingLoop starts with RT, so we need a choice after the first TST1: either enter the re-testing loop or skip it. TestingPhase = TST1 -> AfterTST1 where AfterTST1 = XOR ( TestingLoop, tau )

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 7. After PK, we have LB in cases 1,3,4,5, but in case 2, LB is skipped. So, we have a choice: either do LB or skip it (with a silent transition).

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Putting it all together: Step1 = OR -> MS -> QS -> AS ReworkLoop = * ( IQC, AS ) AfterStep1 = XOR ( ReworkLoop, tau ) TestingPhase = TST -> AfterTST1 AfterPK = XOR ( LB, tau ) Then DP -> D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets verify with the cases: Case 1: ... Case 2: ... Case 3: ... Case 4: ... Case 5: ... So, the model fits all cases.
